title: aegiphila hirsuta var. hirsuta
intro: Aegiphila hirsuta var. hirsuta, also known as the hairy aegiphila, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The hairy aegiphila plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, while also providing relief from digestive problems and skin irritations. Hairy aegiphila can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various methods of harnessing its medicinal benefits.
